title: Parsonsia caeciliae
taxonomy: Parsonsia caeciliae is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. It is a member of the genus Parsonsia, which contains around 30 species of climbing plants native to Australia and New Guinea. The Apocynaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants, with over 5,000 known species. Parsonsia caeciliae is a unique species found in New Guinea, characterized by its distinctive flowers and climbing habit. Its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other plants in the Apocynaceae family.

intro: Parsonsia caeciliae, also known as the Waiananai tree or Waiananai, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. The Waiananai tree has been traditionally used in Polynesian medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, such as caecilioside A and B, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the Waiananai tree is often used to make infusions, teas, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory issues, fever, and skin conditions.
